Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or significant renal pathology. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent clinical evaluation for renal dysfunction and further assessment of hypoglycemia etiology.
